Title: Seed-specific RNAi in safflower generates a superhigh oleic oil with extended oxidative stability

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: S317 (for cloned fragment/background HO transcript analysis)
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: high oleic advanced breeding line; field-tested events in HO background including Event 26 and Event 33
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: omega-6 oleate desaturase / oleoyl phosphatidylcholine desaturase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oleic acid to linoleic acid desaturation on phosphatidylcholine in the endoplasmic reticulum
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces a second double bond into oleate on phosphatidylcholine, converting oleic acid to linoleic acid.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream branch conversion of oleic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: competes with and drains flux away from the target product oleic acid
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for residual linoleic acid production in high-oleic safflower seed; major contributor to remaining seed 18:2 in HO background
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: high oxidative stability oil for food and oleochemical applications
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific RNAi knockdown of CtFAD2.2 markedly reduces linoleic acid to ~1–1.5% and increases seed oleic acid to ~90–94% when combined with CtFATB silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in developing seeds/embryos of HO safflower at 10–20 DAF; transcript abundance unchanged between LO and HO
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: markedly reduced in developing embryos of SHO lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seed stage; multisite field conditions across Australia
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing seeds (Illumina HiSeq2000); small RNA sequencing of maturing seed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: direct editing/inactivation of FAD2 could alter nonseed lipid profiles and crop performance; seed-specific RNAi avoided detectable leaf/root lipid changes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific hairpin RNAi; qRT-PCR; small RNA sequencing; seed fatty acid profiling; multisite field trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtFAD2.2 remained expressed in developing seeds of HO safflower and was identified as the likely source of residual linoleic acid. 2. Seed-specific hairpin RNAi reduced CtFAD2.2 transcript in SHO embryos and generated target-matching small RNAs. 3. Silencing CtFAD2.2 together with CtFATB produced field-stable superhigh oleic oil with ~93% oleic acid and greatly improved oxidative stability.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target for seed-specific suppression to elevate oleic acid while preserving vegetative membrane lipid composition; useful for stacking with palmitate-reducing strategies such as CtFATB silencing.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CtFAD2.1 is the background high-oleic mutation in the HO parent, but CtFAD2.2 is the experimentally manipulated residual desaturase in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: high oleic advanced breeding line; field-tested events in HO background including Event 26 and Event 33
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: acyl-acyl carrier protein thioesterase B / palmitoyl-ACP thioesterase
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: de novo fatty acid synthesis and export for seed oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: chloroplast palmitate export branch supplying cytoplasmic seed oil assembly
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes palmitoyl-ACP in the plastid to release free palmitate for export to the cytoplasm and incorporation into oil.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream precursor-export branch from chloroplast fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: diverts flux away from oleic acid accumulation by exporting 16:0; reducing it decreases saturates and favors oleic enrichment
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point for palmitic acid accumulation in seed oil; not established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: high oxidative stability oil for food and oleochemical applications
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-specific RNAi knockdown of CtFATB reduces palmitic acid from ~5% to ~2% and contributes to increased flux toward oleic acid in SHO seed oil.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in developing embryos; transcript abundance unchanged between LO and HO
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: markedly reduced in developing embryos of SHO lines
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: developing seed stage; multisite field conditions across Australia
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq of developing seeds (Illumina HiSeq2000); small RNA sequencing of maturing seed
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: seed profile change may affect seedling vigour under some field conditions; direct editing/inactivation could alter nonseed lipid profiles, whereas seed-specific RNAi minimized such effects
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: de novo transcript discovery; seed-specific hairpin RNAi; qRT-PCR; small RNA sequencing; seed fatty acid profiling; field trials
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A single FATB-like transcript, CtFATB, was identified from developing safflower embryos. 2. Seed-specific RNAi reduced CtFATB expression and produced corresponding small RNAs in SHO seeds. 3. Combined silencing with CtFAD2.2 lowered palmitic acid to ~2% and supported stable ~93% oleic oil in field-grown seed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful companion engineering target with FAD2 suppression to reduce saturates while maximizing oleic acid without major nonseed lipid disruption when silenced seed-specifically.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors describe FATB as transporting/exporting 16:0 from chloroplast to cytoplasm via thioesterase activity; study focuses on dual RNAi with CtFAD2.2 rather than FATB alone.
